steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes establish one key difference between the two cartographic traditions: Venetian portolan charts were sparse in illustrations, while Majorcan ones were richly illustrated. The correct choice must accurately state this contrast without reversing or distorting the facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Unlike Venetian portolan charts, which could include illustrations of palm trees and shepherds' crooks, Majorcan charts were sparse in illustration.: attributes rich illustration to Venetian charts and sparseness to Majorcan ones, which is the opposite of what the notes say. ✗
  • Choice B — Being in the Majorcan tradition, the portolan charts of Gabriel de Vallseca differed from those of the Venetian cartographer Domenico Pizigano.: describes a feature shared by both traditions rather than the distinction between them. ✗
  • Choice C — While portolan charts in the Majorcan tradition tended to be richly illustrated, those in the Venetian tradition mapped the waterways of the Mediterranean and Black Seas.: wrongly assigns a general characteristic to only the Venetian tradition. ✗
  • Choice D — Majorcan portolan charts, unlike their sparser Venetian counterparts, tended to be richly illustrated.: states the contrast directly and accurately as the notes describe it. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
